message:
perf(raptorq/bench): deterministic scenario matrix with bit-exactness validation

Overhaul GF(256) primitive benchmarks to use a deterministic scenario
matrix (Track E artifact alignment) with built-in correctness validation,
replacing the previous ad-hoc size-based iteration.

- Add Gf256BenchScenario struct with scenario_id, seed, k, symbol_size,
  loss_pattern, len, and mul_const for reproducible profiling
- Add gf256_scenarios() returning 5 canonical scenarios (RQ-E-GF256-001
  through 005) spanning 64B to 16KiB with varying multiplier constants
- Add validate_gf256_bit_exactness() that runs reference scalar
  implementations against the dispatched SIMD-capable implementations
  for add_slice, mul_slice, and addmul_slice before every benchmark
  iteration — catches correctness regressions at profile time
- Add deterministic_bytes() xorshift64-based PRNG for reproducible input
- Add reference_mul_slice() and reference_addmul_slice() as independent
  scalar reference implementations for cross-checking
- Benchmark IDs now include scenario_id, length, seed, k, and symbol_size
  for unambiguous identification in criterion reports
- Context strings include artifact_path and repro_cmd for traceability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
